questions.question: Which of the following is a tautology?
questions.explanation: p ∨ ¬p is the Law of Excluded Middle — it is always TRUE regardless of p's truth value. p ∧ ¬p is always FALSE (contradiction). p → q depends on values of p and q.

questions.question: In a group of 100 students, 60 study Maths, 50 study Physics, and 20 study both. How many study neither?
The number of bijective functions from {1,2,3} to {a,b,c} is
questions.explanation: The number of bijective (one-to-one, onto) functions between two n-element sets is n! = 3! = 6.

questions.question: Which relation on {1,2,3} is an equivalence relation?
questions.explanation: Option A: Reflexive (1,1),(2,2),(3,3) ✓; Symmetric (1,2)↔(2,1) ✓; Transitive (1,2),(2,1)→(1,1) ✓. This is an equivalence relation. Option C lacks (2,1) so not symmetric.

questions.question: Which of the following is logically equivalent to ¬(p → q)?
questions.explanation: p → q ≡ ¬p ∨ q. Therefore ¬(p → q) ≡ ¬(¬p ∨ q) ≡ p ∧ ¬q (De Morgan's law). This means the implication fails exactly when p is true and q is false. Option B is equivalent to p → q itself (not its negation).

questions.question: The power set of {a, b, c} has how many elements?
questions.explanation: The power set P(A) of a set A with n elements has 2ⁿ elements. For A = {a,b,c}, n=3, so |P(A)| = 2³ = 8. The 8 subsets are: ∅, {a}, {b}, {c}, {a,b}, {a,c}, {b,c}, {a,b,c}.

questions.question: The number of ways to arrange the letters of the word 'GATE' is
questions.explanation: GATE has 4 distinct letters (G, A, T, E). Number of arrangements = 4! = 4×3×2×1 = 24. If there were repeated letters, we would divide by the factorial of the repeat count.

questions.question: Boolean expression A·(A + B) simplifies to
questions.explanation: Using Boolean absorption law: A·(A + B) = A·A + A·B = A + A·B = A(1 + B) = A·1 = A. Alternatively, A·(A+B): if A=0, result=0; if A=1, result=1·(1+B)=1. So result equals A in both cases.

questions.question: How many integers from 1 to 100 are divisible by 3 or 5?
questions.explanation: By inclusion-exclusion: |A∪B| = |A|+|B|-|A∩B|. Divisible by 3: floor(100/3)=33. Divisible by 5: floor(100/5)=20. Divisible by 15: floor(100/15)=6. Total = 33+20-6 = 47.

questions.question: A partial order relation must be
questions.explanation: A partial order (poset) satisfies: (1) Reflexive: aRa for all a; (2) Antisymmetric: aRb and bRa implies a=b; (3) Transitive: aRb and bRc implies aRc. Example: ≤ on integers. If also every pair is comparable, it becomes a total order. An equivalence relation is reflexive + symmetric + transitive.

questions.question: The number of strings of length 3 over the alphabet {0, 1} is
questions.explanation: Each position has 2 choices (0 or 1), and positions are independent. Total strings = 2×2×2 = 2³ = 8. They are: 000, 001, 010, 011, 100, 101, 110, 111. In general, strings of length n over alphabet of size k = kⁿ.

questions.question: The Boolean expression (A + B)(A + B') simplifies to
questions.explanation: Expand: (A+B)(A+B') = A·A + A·B' + B·A + B·B' = A + A·B' + A·B + 0 = A + A(B'+B) = A + A·1 = A + A = A. Alternatively using distributivity: A+(B·B') = A+0 = A. The key is B·B'=0.

questions.question: By the pigeonhole principle, if 13 balls are placed into 12 boxes, at least one box contains
questions.explanation: Pigeonhole Principle: if n+1 objects are placed into n containers, at least one container holds at least 2 objects. Here 13 balls into 12 boxes: at least one box has ≥ 2 balls. Note: it guarantees 'at least 2', not 'exactly 2' (one box could have all 13).

questions.question: The contrapositive of 'If it rains, then the ground is wet' is

options.A: If the ground is not wet, then it does not rain
options.B: If it does not rain, then the ground is not wet
options.C: If the ground is wet, then it rains
options.D: It rains and the ground is not wet

questions.explanation: The contrapositive of p → q is ¬q → ¬p. Here p = 'it rains', q = 'ground is wet'. Contrapositive: ¬q → ¬p = 'If ground is not wet → it does not rain'. The contrapositive is logically equivalent to the original statement. Converse (q→p) and inverse (¬p→¬q) are NOT equivalent to the original.
